items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1000):
items.body: World-class ball striker and betting favorite this week, coming off a strong Florida Swing and ranking near the top of true strokes gained tee-to-green in this field.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+1400):
items.body: Elite all-around game and one of the best course fits at Copperhead, with a prior top-5 here and a runner-up finish at THE PLAYERS last week signaling peak form.

items.label: Viktor Hovland (+1600):
items.body: Defending Valspar champion and recent Florida standout, known for precise iron play and improved short game that travels well on demanding, tree-lined layouts.

items.label: Brooks Koepka (+2200):
items.body: Major champion with winning experience on tough, par-71 setups; his high-end ceiling and comfort in big-time fields make him a high-upside outright target.

items.label: Justin Thomas (+2000):
items.body: Coming off a strong showing at THE PLAYERS, his creativity and wedge play make him dangerous on Copperhead’s small, contoured greens.

items.label: Patrick Cantlay (+2500):
items.body: Hyper-consistent tee-to-green and one of the most reliable fairway finders in the field, setting up plenty of mid-iron birdie looks on a demanding track.

items.label: Jordan Spieth (+2800):
items.body: Former winner at Copperhead with an excellent history on the Snake Pit, and his streaky putter always gives him win equity on tough scoring weeks.

items.label: Corey Conners (+3000):
items.body: Elite ball striker in this setup, frequently ranking near the top in strokes gained on approach and well-suited to a second-shot course like Copperhead.

items.label: Ryo Hisatsune (+4000):
items.body: Trending as a breakout candidate with a top-5 here last year and multiple recent top-20 finishes, backed by excellent tee-to-green metrics this season.

items.label: End of Florida Swing:
items.body: The Valspar Championship closes out the PGA Tour’s four-event Florida Swing, giving players one last look at bermudagrass and coastal winds before the schedule shifts.

items.label: Elite Field at Copperhead:
items.body: Seven of the top-20 players in the world are in the field, including Schauffele, Hovland, Fitzpatrick, Thomas, and Spieth, creating one of the stronger Valspar fields in recent years.

items.label: Defending Champion Pressure:
items.body: Viktor Hovland returns to defend his Valspar title after edging Justin Thomas by one shot last year, testing his accuracy again on one of the Tour’s most demanding shot-makers’ courses.

items.label: Model and Market Favorites:
items.body: Betting markets and projection models are tightly clustered at the top, with Schauffele, Fitzpatrick, and Hovland sharing short odds and significant win equity in simulations.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Viktor Hovland is the reigning Valspar winner after his narrow victory over Justin Thomas last year, adding to a list of past champions who thrive on precision and patience.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: Copperhead has historically rewarded elite ball striking rather than pure distance, with several champions and contenders ranking highly in strokes gained tee-to-green for the week.

items.label: Upset Winners:
items.body: Under-the-radar names have broken through here in the past, with multiple champions going off at 90-1 or longer, keeping long-shot outright tickets firmly in play.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Innisbrook Resort (Copperhead Course), Palm Harbor, Florida; Par 71 / 7,352 yards, a tree-lined, positional test featuring elevation changes and the famous three-hole "Snake Pit" closing stretch.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $9.1 million total purse, with the champion’s share just over $1.6 million and a significant FedEx Cup points haul on offer.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-hole stroke play with a standard 36-hole cut to the low 65 and ties after Friday’s second round.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: Tournament rounds run Thursday, March 19 through Sunday, March 22, aligning with the heart of the 2026 PGA Tour regular season.

items.label: TV:
items.body: Golf Channel and NBC provide coverage in the U.S., with featured groups and early coverage available via PGA Tour Live on ESPN+.

items.label: Thursday, March 19:
items.body: Early streaming coverage in the morning on PGA Tour Live, with TV window typically in the mid-afternoon Eastern Time.

items.label: Friday, March 20:
items.body: Second-round coverage mirrors Thursday with streaming in the morning and TV coverage in the afternoon Eastern Time.

items.label: Saturday, March 21:
items.body: Moving-day broadcast windows focus on featured pairings and the Snake Pit, with afternoon TV coverage on NBC and streaming all day.

items.label: Sunday, March 22:
items.body: Final-round coverage highlights leaders navigating the Snake Pit, with an afternoon TV window on NBC and full-round streaming on PGA Tour Live.
